phases.label: build_hot_orderkey_set
phases.description: Full parallel scan of lineitem (l_orderkey + l_quantity). Build thread-local hash maps {orderkey -> sum_qty}, merge, filter sum_qty > 300.0 to produce hot_keys set.

phases.label: point_lookup_join_aggregate
phases.description: For each hot orderkey: probe orders_orderkey_hash for order row, probe customer_custkey_hash for customer row, binary-search lineitem_orderkey_sorted for lineitem rows and accumulate SUM(l_quantity). Emit one result row per hot orderkey.
phases.output: result_rows: vector<{c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice, sum_qty}>

parallelism.partitioning: hot_keys split into nthread chunks, each thread processes its chunk independently

phases.label: sort_and_limit
phases.description: In-memory sort of ~3000 result rows by o_totalprice DESC, o_orderdate ASC. Emit top 100.

parallelism.notes: Result cardinality ~3000 rows; sort overhead negligible

join_order.notes: Phase 1 subquery: scan all 60M lineitem rows, aggregate by l_orderkey, filter SUM > 300 → hot_keys set of ~3000 entries
O(1) hash probe per hot orderkey into pre-built orders PK hash index; ~3000 probes total
O(1) hash probe per qualifying order using o_custkey into pre-built customer PK hash index
Binary search into lineitem_orderkey_sorted (60M pairs sorted by l_orderkey) per hot key; fetch row_ids, accumulate l_quantity

data_structures.aggregation: none_needed (each hot orderkey maps to exactly one orders row; grouping key unique per orderkey)

indexes.purpose: O(1) PK lookup of orders row by o_orderkey
O(1) PK lookup of customer row by c_custkey
Binary-search range scan of lineitem rows by l_orderkey for phase 2 quantity aggregation
